name: Ken Kersey
abstract: Kenneth Lyons Kersey (April 3, 1916 – April 1, 1983) was a Canadian jazz pianist who spent most of his life working in the United States.

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Sidney asked if I'd go in with him with a quartet, the wonderful Kenny Kersey on piano and Vic Dickenson on trombone, and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Going back to your days in Boston and these musicians, Vic Dickenson and Edmund Hall and all them – was Ken Kersey there also? [INTERVIEWEE] Oh yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: the band personnel started out, Buck Clayton on trumpet, Bennie Morton on trombone, Eddie Barefield, and Ken Kersey.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: I think that's when Ken Kersey left the band... Bud Powell taken Ken Kersey's place... Ken Kersey was all right.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: they brought in a much better piano player, Ken Kersey, but I could have played that – and I wanted to.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: That was Lester and Coleman Hawkins and myself and Helen Humes, Kenny Kersey on piano.
